commit.message:
fix: replace azuredevops_agent_queues with azuredevops_agent_queue data source

The plural data source does not exist in the azuredevops provider.
Switch to the singular azuredevops_agent_queue which looks up by name.

Since the singular data source errors if the queue doesn't exist,
introduce a boolean variable ado_queue_exists (default: false).
- First run / new project: ado_queue_exists=false → resource created normally
- Re-run with lost state: ado_queue_exists=true → data source used, resource skipped

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace broken JsonSerializerContext emission with JsonSerializerOptions

Source generators can't see each other's output, so emitting
partial class Xxx : JsonSerializerContext with [JsonSerializable]
attributes never worked in real projects — the STJ source gen
never processed them.

Changed all 3 emitters (JsonContext, ModuleJsonContext, ChainOfThought)
to emit static JsonSerializerOptions helpers instead. Fixed sample
to use source-generated GetPredictors()/CloneCore() instead of
hand-written reflection hacks. Sample now runs E2E with source gen.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix yield* return delegation to inner iterator

Three issues fixed:

1. The yield* SetupTry handler was marked as catch-only (flags=0), so
   handle_throw skipped it when generator_return_pending was set. Changed
   to for_finally=true so the delegation handler is always entered.

2. Added ClearGeneratorReturn opcode emitted at the start of the return
   delegation path. This allows errors thrown during return delegation
   (e.g., inner .return() throwing, returning non-object, getter errors)
   to be caught by user catch handlers instead of being skipped.

3. In the 'return is undefined' path, restore generator_return_pending
   via SetGeneratorReturn before throwing so the return completion
   properly propagates through finally handlers.

Fixes all 10 yield* return delegation tests in cluster 15.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix initializer-safe bulk renaming and NameFixPass initializer iteration (#369)

## Summary

Fixes onnx/ir-py#353.

This PR addresses two related rename failures:

1. Direct rename loops fail for initializer-backed values in
swap/permutation cases.
2. `NameFixPass` can raise `RuntimeError: dictionary keys changed during
iteration` when a rename changes initializer dict keys while the pass is
iterating them.

## Changes

- add `onnx_ir.convenience.rename_values()` for bulk value renaming
- make initializer-backed multi-value renames safe by using temporary
names during swaps/permutations
- keep the existing initializer collision checks for names outside the
rename set
- make `NameFixPass` iterate over a snapshot of initializer values
instead of the live dict view
- add regression tests for:
  - swapping two initializer-backed outputs
- renaming to an existing initializer name before running `NameFixPass`

## Why

A single `value.name = ...` rename is not enough for safe initializer
swaps, because the initializer name guard correctly rejects taking
another live initializer name. The new bulk helper provides the missing
multi-rename operation, while the `NameFixPass` change makes renaming
safe when initializer dict keys are updated during the pass.

## Validation

- `pytest -q src/onnx_ir/_convenience/_convenience_test.py -k
rename_values`
- `pytest -q src/onnx_ir/passes/common/naming_test.py -k
initializer_collision`

Reported from downstream integration work in jax2onnx.

---------

Signed-off-by: enpasos <matthias.unverzagt@enpasos.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Justin Chu <justinchuby@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: agents use ask_user tool for clarification — no hardcoded menus

The LLM now drives ALL interactive disambiguation decisions.
Hardcoded Python input() menus and if-len>1 branching are replaced by
the ask_user tool, which the LLM calls only when genuine ambiguity exists.

tools/ask_user_tool.py (new)
- ask_user(question, options?) pauses the agent loop and collects user
  input; returns {answer, index} as a tool result back to the LLM
- TOOL_DEFINITION in Groq function-calling schema format
- Design note included: agents must never bypass this with raw input()

agents/exchange_disambiguator.py (refactored)
- Now a proper BaseAgent subclass with tools=[ASK_USER_TOOL]
- LLM system prompt instructs it to auto-select when obvious, and call
  ask_user when: multiple distinct companies OR multiple exchanges found
- Removed: _llm_auto_select, _resolve_single_company, _resolve_multiple_companies,
  _ask_exchange, _ask_to_refine (all replaced by the LLM tool loop)
- Kept: _group_by_company, _best_per_exchange, _find_group_for_ticker,
  _normalise_name (preprocessing helpers fed to the LLM as context)

orchestrator.py
- Removed _pick_company() static method (was hardcoded company menu)
- Removed Step B.5 company disambiguation call
- Removed _normalise_name import (no longer needed here)
- All candidate lists are passed to ExchangeDisambiguatorAgent as before;
  the agent's LLM loop handles both company AND exchange selection

.github/copilot-instructions.md
- Added Pattern 7: Agent Clarification Pattern (ask_user tool)
- Rule documented: no raw input() or hardcoded menus inside agents
- Updated Conventions: interactive prompts via ask_user only; only
  main.py may use raw input()
- Updated architecture diagram and file structure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement comprehensive authentication system with Google Sign-In and onboarding

Features implemented:
- AuthViewModel with state management (AuthState sealed class)
- AuthenticationRepository centralizing Firebase operations
- Email/password auth with robust validation
- Google Sign-In integration with OAuth credentials
- OnboardingFragment for post-registration profile collection
- Password recovery functionality
- Session persistence with DataStore
- Comprehensive error handling with Italian user messages
- Material Design 3 UI with proper loading states
- Navigation graph updated with onboarding flow
- DI pattern using AuthViewModelProvider interface

Technical details:
- Uses Kotlin Coroutines and StateFlow for async operations
- Dual persistence: DataStore for preferences, Room for durable data
- ActivityResultContract for safe Google Sign-In
- Password strength validation (8+ chars, uppercase, number)
- Onboarding data validation (name, weight 30-300kg, height 100-250cm)
- All Firebase exceptions mapped to user-friendly messages

Files created:
- AuthViewModel.kt
- AuthenticationRepository.kt
- OnboardingFragment.kt
- fragment_onboarding.xml
- ic_google.xml
- edit_text_background.xml
- AUTHENTICATION_IMPLEMENTATION.md (comprehensive docs)

Files modified:
- AuthFragment.kt (complete rewrite with Google SignIn)
- MainActivity.kt (DI implementation)
- fragment_auth.xml (enhanced layout)
- nav_graph.xml (onboarding actions)
- build.gradle.kts (added Google Play Services Auth)
- strings.xml (40+ error/UI strings)

Note: Build requires Java 11, 17, or 21 (Java 26 has Kotlin compiler compatibility issues)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove as T assertions, fix page.evaluate serialization, add sight tests

- Change collectClickableElementIds signature to (elements, boundary, getComputedStyle)
  eliminating the `as unknown as Parameters<...>[0]` type assertion
- Export ElementLike type so tests can import it directly
- Revert sight() to fully inline element-collection logic; calling module-level
  collectClickableElementIds from within sight() would ReferenceError in the browser
  because page.evaluate() only serializes the function body, not module-level defs
- Replace `as 'buy' | 'sell'` assertion with a type-safe local parseBulkMode function
- Add exported SightRawData type and buildSightResult(data) pure function to enable
  unit testing of sight's data-transformation logic without a browser environment
- Update server.test.ts: adjust collectClickableElementIds call sites to new signature,
  add buildSightResult tests (number parsing, bulk mode, statistics, passthrough fields)

* fix: guard checkVisibility against absence in older browser builds

Make checkVisibility optional in ElementLike and use optional-chaining
with === false in collectClickableElementIds so elements are treated as
visible when the API is unavailable. In sight(), add a local isVisible
helper with a typeof guard for the same reason. Add a test covering the
fallback (element without checkVisibility is included).

Create KQL stored functions and ontology graph in Eventhouse

- ENTITIES + RELATIONSHIPS tables for ontology graph
- Broker→Fund→Holding→UBO ownership chains (3 exchanges, 30 brokers)
- detect_spoofing(), detect_layering(), detect_wash_trading(),
  detect_anomalies(), detect_all() stored functions
- resolve_ubo() and get_regulations() helper functions
- Wash trading uses graph-based UBO resolution via KQL joins
- Cross-market UBO chains enable wash trade detection across SGX/HKEX/NSE
- kql/stored_functions.kql + kql/ontology_tables.kql for version control
- setup-ontology.sh populates 65+ entities and 59 relationships
- deploy-stored-functions.sh deploys all 7 functions to KQL DB
- setup-eventhouse.sh orchestrates full Eventhouse provisioning
- init-kql-tables.sh updated with ENTITIES and RELATIONSHIPS tables

Note: Eventhouse creation via Fabric REST API returns FeatureNotAvailable
(tenant capacity delegation). Create surveillance-eh via Fabric portal,
then run: scripts/setup-eventhouse.sh

commit.message:
feat: ask user to select company when multiple distinct companies found

- Orchestrator._pick_company(): groups CompanySearchAgent results by
  normalised name so ADR/GDR/Old variants of the SAME company collapse
  into a single entry. Only truly distinct companies (e.g. 'Infosys
  Limited' vs 'Infosys BPM Limited') are shown as separate choices.
- Inserted Step B.5 in _resolve(): calls _pick_company() after Step B
  so the user picks the company BEFORE ticker searches run. This keeps
  the subsequent exchange menu focused on one company.
- Import _normalise_name from exchange_disambiguator into orchestrator
  so both use identical normalisation logic.
- _normalise_name: also strips parenthetical qualifiers (GDR), (Old),
  (ADR) so grouping is even more accurate.
- _find_group_for_ticker() added to ExchangeDisambiguatorAgent: after
  LLM auto-selects a ticker it looks up the company group; if that group
  has multiple exchange listings the exchange menu is shown instead of
  silently returning the LLM pick.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): revert arguments DCE + remove dead code + add array push IC

- Revert CreateMappedArguments/CreateUnmappedArguments/CreateRestParameter
  DCE: removing these nodes from the graph changes register allocation
  and exposes latent regalloc bugs on Linux release builds (SIGSEGV,
  wrong results). They are now treated as side-effecting again. Codegen
  already handles them as cheap no-ops when they appear in the graph.

- Remove unused decode_jit_values function (clippy dead_code error).

- Add CallProp1IcEntry single-entry IC for arr.push() in CallProperty1
  dispatch. Caches callee handle to skip PropertyMap lookup on hot path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Table removed — all info accessible via the DAG + detail panel.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix proxy has trap fallback to use has_property_in_chain

When a Proxy has no 'has' trap, the fallback check for [[HasProperty]]
used lookup_proto_chain which doesn't check accessor patterns (__get_/
__set_). This caused 'key in proxy' to return false for accessor
properties inherited from the prototype chain (e.g., RegExp getters
like ignoreCase, flags, source).

Replace the manual fallback with has_property_in_chain which correctly
handles own properties, accessors, __type__-based prototype resolution,
and full prototype chain traversal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Batch-translate all words via single AOAI call on text load

Replace per-word Azure Translator API calls with a single GPT-4o-mini
call that translates all unique words at once using full text context.

- translationService: new batchTranslateText() sends unique words +
  full text to GPT, returns Map<word, translation>
- ReadingSession: calls batchTranslateText on mount and language change,
  passes pre-computed map to WordPopup
- WordPopup: instant lookup from map (no API call, no loading state)
  Shows 'Translating...' only while batch call is in flight

Benefits: 1 API call instead of N, instant word popups, better context.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: add GitHub Actions workflow for lint, test, and build

Three jobs:
- lint: ruff check on src/ and tests/
- test: pytest on Python 3.12 and 3.13 (needs lint)
- build: package build + wheel install verification (needs lint)

commit.message:
feat: add multi-provider LLM support (Anthropic + OpenAI)

Introduce an LLMClient protocol and factory pattern so the agent can
use either Anthropic Claude (via Azure Foundry) or OpenAI GPT models.

Key changes:
- agent/llm.py: LLMClient Protocol, rename ClaudeClient → AnthropicClient
  (alias preserved), create_llm_client() factory
- agent/openai_llm.py: new OpenAIClient — Chat Completions API format,
  OpenAI vision/streaming, retry logic
- config.py: llm_provider field (anthropic/openai), OpenAIConfig model,
  env var overrides, conditional credential validation
- orchestrator.py: uses factory instead of direct ClaudeClient()
- Type annotations updated across core.py, vision.py, summarizer.py,
  context.py to use LLMClient protocol
- Full test coverage: 28 OpenAI client tests, factory tests, config tests
- README updated with provider comparison and configuration docs

No breaking changes — defaults to 'anthropic', ClaudeClient alias kept.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove worker containers — detection moves to Fabric RTI

Detection logic now runs natively in Fabric:
- KQL stored functions for spoofing/layering/wash/anomaly detection
- Data Activator Reflex triggers for autonomous intervention
- Ontology graph in Eventhouse for UBO resolution

Dashboard remains as sole Container App for UI and simulation demos.
Python agent library retained for local testing and demo purposes.

Removed:
- src/worker/ (worker.py, Dockerfile, requirements.txt)
- infra/modules/worker-app.bicep
- Worker module references in main.bicep
- Live worker Container Apps from Azure

commit.message:
fix: mute immediately overridden by reconnect unmute sync

_sync_unmute_to_ma() fired on every sink_muted=False status update from
the daemon subprocess, not just after a (re)start. When mute was routed
through MA, the PA sink stayed unmuted, causing the sync to instantly
reverse the user's mute command.

Added _pending_reconnect_unmute_sync flag: set True on subprocess start,
consumed (set False) after the first unmute sync. Subsequent
sink_muted=False events no longer trigger MA unmute.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix boost not showing in Popular Now - add boosted logic to search handler

The Flutter fetchPopularUsers() sends min_age, max_age, and radius as
query parameters. hasSearchFilters() detects these and routes the request
to handleSearchWithFilters() instead of generateRecommendations(). But
handleSearchWithFilters() had NO boosted user logic — no country-wide
fetch, no re-adding of excluded boosted users.

Added the same boosted user prioritization to handleSearchWithFilters():
- Fetch active boosted users from same country
- Re-fetch boosted users excluded by distance/interaction filters
- Serialize them in proper stdClass format
- Place boosted users first in results

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
